rounds_summary.attempted_route: Track 5 candidate functionals (C1=int u, C2=int v, C3=int uv, C4=quadratic energy 1/2 int(u^2+v^2+v_x^2), C5=int m^2 with m=u-v^2/2) on soliton IC v0=1.5 sech^2(x+5), u0=0; Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + IMEX-CN on v_xxx + midpoint RK2, Nx=256, dt=2.5e-4, T=20.
rounds_summary.observation: C1, C2 conserved at FFT round-off (~1e-15); C3 drifts to 0.54 with excursions ~1.8; C4 grows 119% monotonically; C5 grows 724% monotonically; no blow-up, sup-norm ~3-4. C1's zero start is ambiguous (u0=0).

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Same solver; change ONLY IC to smooth multi-mode periodic with nonzero means (u0=0.4 sin(2pi x/L)+0.2 cos(6pi x/L)+0.15, v0=0.6 cos(2pi x/L)+0.3 sin(4pi x/L)+0.10), T=10, to disambiguate structural vs IC-specific conservation and test IC-invariance of drifts.
rounds_summary.observation: C1 stays at 4.5 to 1.6e-14, C2 at 3.0 to 3.6e-15 (structurally conserved, not IC-specific). C3, C4, C5 drift faster: C4 grows 16x, C5 grows 34x. By-hand IBP shows d/dt(1/2 int(u^2+v^2)) = +(5/2)int u_x v^2 + int u_x v_xx, a non-vanishing cubic residual.

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Numerical-artifact control on E1 IC: three configs at T=5 — A (dt=5e-4, Nx=256), B (dt=1e-4, Nx=256), C (dt=2.5e-4, Nx=512). A vs B isolates dt-only effect; ratio ~ 5^p tests order-p artifact.
rounds_summary.observation: drift_A/drift_B = 1.002, 0.992, 0.996 for C3, C4, C5 (inferred order p ~ 0). 5x dt change shifts drift by <1%. Nx 256->512 modulates drift by 2-15%, not order-of-magnitude. C1, C2 stay at machine zero across all configs. Drift is physical, not numerical.

ruled_out_routes: Quadratic energy ansatz E = 1/2 int(u^2 + v^2 + v_x^2) dx as a BKdV invariant — grows 119% on soliton IC and 1570% on multi-mode IC, dt-invariant; IBP proves +(5/2)int u_x v^2 cubic remainder cannot be cancelled by quadratic counterterms.
Cross moment int u v dx as an invariant — drifts O(1) with large oscillations on both ICs, dt-invariant; no conservation structure.
||m||^2 = int (u - v^2/2)^2 dx as an invariant — monotonically grows whenever IC has m != 0; m=0 is a Gardner reduction manifold, not a stable invariant of the full BKdV flow.

Searching for 'slowly drifting near-conserved' quantities within the C1..C5 candidate set — the split is binary (machine-zero conservation for C1, C2 vs O(1) drift for C3, C4, C5); no candidate in this set occupies the slow-drift category.
Attributing the C3/C4/C5 drift to dt/Nx numerical artifact — A/B dt-ratio test gives implied order p ~ 0 (<1% drift change for 5x dt change), ruling out timestep/dealias error as the source.
synthesised_diagnosis: The 5-candidate set splits binarily by mechanism. C1=int u dx and C2=int v dx are exactly conserved at FFT round-off across all ICs and all (dt, Nx) configs — trivially, because both PDEs are in divergence form (u_t=-d/dx(...), v_t=-d/dx(...)) on a periodic domain. C3, C4, C5 exhibit O(1) physical non-conservation that is IC-invariant and dt-invariant (drift ratio ~1.000 under 5x dt change), so the drift is not numerical. The quadratic 'sum of squared fields' ansatz is structurally not the BKdV Hamiltonian — IBP yields an uncancellable cubic remainder +(5/2)int u_x v^2 + int u_x v_xx — and ||m||^2 measures distance from a reduction manifold rather than a true invariant. No 'slowly drifting' quantity exists in this candidate set.
rationale: The candidate functionals were drawn from naive quadratic/cross-moment guesses; the only survivors (C1, C2) are tautological divergence-form mass invariants. The true non-trivial BKdV invariants (if any) require cubic/derivative-coupling terms not present in the quadratic ansatz, as shown by the IBP residual.
recommended_alternative: Search the BKdV Hamiltonian among cubic-coupled functionals that can cancel the +(5/2)int u_x v^2 + int u_x v_xx residual: try H = int (1/2 v_x^2 - v^3 - alpha u v^2 - beta u_x v_xx + gamma u^2) dx with coefficients (alpha, beta, gamma) fit by enforcing d/dt H = 0 symbolically (sympy) and verified numerically with the same Fourier+IMEX-CN solver (Nx=256, dt=2.5e-4) on both E1 soliton and E2 multi-mode ICs; also test the known KdV-side P3 = int(v_x^2/2 - v^3)dx alone to confirm whether the v-sector retains its standard KdV cubic invariant when coupled to u.
trivial_reason: C1 and C2 conservation sub-claims within all three findings are trivial (divergence-form mass on periodic domain), but the overall 3-round investigation is non-trivial: it screens the candidate set, certifies the drifts as physical (not numerical) via dt-scaling, and produces an analytic IBP obstruction showing the quadratic energy ansatz is structurally wrong.
